description:
Dead-fn elimination pass that removes FnIrs unreachable from `main` after inlining.

The inliner leaves callee FnIrs in module.fns even after all their callsites inline away; codegen still compiles them, wasting cycles and code space.

## What 'reachable' means in fz-IR

Starting from `main` FnIr:
  - Follow Term::Call.callee → FnId
  - Follow Term::TailCall.callee → FnId
  - Follow Term::Call.continuation.fn_id → FnId
  - Follow Term::CallClosure.continuation.fn_id → FnId
  - Follow Prim::MakeClosure(FnId, _) → FnId

Mark all reached FnIds; sweep the rest. Also mark referenced ExternIds; sweep unreferenced externs.

## Two commits

C1: add dce_module_level(m: &mut Module) to ir_dce.rs
    - BFS from main via the traversal above
    - module.fns.retain(|f| reachable.contains(&f.id))
    - rebuild fn_idx
    - module.externs.retain(|e| referenced_externs.contains(&e.id))
    - FnIds are NOT renumbered (schemas vec is indexed by FnId.0; placeholder slots harmless)

C2: wire into pipeline in ir_codegen.rs after inline_single_use_conts + block-level dce

## Tests
- After inline_module, a fully-inlined leaf callee with 0 remaining callsites is dropped.
- A fn still referenced via Prim::MakeClosure is kept (conservative).
- main is always kept.
- End-to-end: output unchanged.

## Acceptance
- Two commits, each referencing fz-ul4.11.29.
title: Dead-callee elimination pass
